description: A naturally raised area of land, smaller and less steep than a mountain. 'Molira' names the gentle swellings of the landscape—rises one can walk without climbing, vantage points that reveal the surrounding land without demanding ascent.
sound_symbolism: The word rises gently. 'Mo' begins with mounded nasal sound, earth gathered. Then 'li' lifts lightly, the gradual slope. Finally 'ra' reaches the crest, opening to the view beyond.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo molira' (hills). Takes the object classifier 'themo' when counted. Distinguished from 'tomora' (mountain) by gentleness and scale. Often plural in landscape descriptions. Associated with gentle effort, perspective, and pastoral settings.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Hills teach gentle effort—the climb is manageable, the view rewards, the lesson is persistence without strain.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Hills create the gentle curves of pastoral landscape—soft rises that guide the eye through organic terrain.
